description_long: This is a bright red, vintage triplane with three stacked wings, each adorned with black and white Iron Cross insignias, reminiscent of World War I German aircraft. The fuselage is cylindrical and tapers toward the tail, which features a single vertical stabilizer and a rounded horizontal stabilizer, both marked with similar insignias. The nose houses a wooden two-blade propeller and a visible radial piston engine, while the undercarriage consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windshield and a single seat visible. The surface of the airplane appears to be made of painted metal and fabric, with some exposed structural elements. This aircraft is compact and lightweight, designed for agility and short takeoff and landing capabilities.
description: A vivid red, vintage triplane with three stacked wings, Iron Cross insignias, an open cockpit, and a wooden propeller. The design is compact, lightweight, and highly detailed, evoking early 20th-century aviation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the red fuselage, three stacked wings with Iron Cross insignias, open cockpit, and tail details.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the triple wing configuration, full wingspan, and tailplane with insignias.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the propeller, landing gear, and cockpit position, with clear markings on the tail.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the radial engine, wooden propeller, and the layered structure of the three wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Triplane configuration with three straight, mid-mounted wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted radial piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer; rounded edges and classic layout.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a rear skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing with a small windshield.
